cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Anthony could have gone to the amusement park. The key here is to look at his schedule and see what times he was occupied or accounted for, and then find the gaps where he might have been free to go to the park. First, Anthony wakes up at 6am. Then Leslie sees him at the library from 6am to 8am. So from 6 to 8, he was reading. Then Jennifer saw him at the gym working out from 8am to 9am. So 8 to 9 am, he was at the gym. So by 9am, he was done with that. Then there's a time span where Anthony saw Anthony (maybe a mirror or something?) buying cookies at a bakery from 2pm to 9pm. Wait, that's a long time—2pm to 9pm. So during that entire period, Anthony was at the bakery? Maybe that's a typo? But the question says Anthony saw Anthony buying cookies... Maybe it's a typo for someone else? Wait, the original question says "Anthony saw Anthony buying cookies..." That seems odd. Maybe it's a mistake and it's supposed to be another person? But assuming the data is correct as given, then from 2pm to 9pm Anthony was at the bakery. Then William saw Anthony at the bike shop from 9pm to 10pm. The amusement park was closed after 10pm, so Anthony couldn't have gone after that. Let me map this out: - 6am-8am: Library - 8am-9am: Gym - 9am-2pm: Free time (unless there's something else) - 2pm-9pm: Bakery - 9pm-10pm: Bike shop So the only free time Anthony has is between 9am and 2pm. Because after 2pm he's at the bakery until 9pm, and then at the bike shop from 9pm to 10pm. Before 6am, he wakes up at 6, so before that, he's presumably asleep. So between 9am and 2pm, he doesn't have any scheduled activities mentioned. That would be the window when he could have gone to the amusement park. Now the options are: (A)
